EF0 DAMAGE WAS FOUND IN THE CITY OF HOLLY POND, TOUCHING DOWN JUST SW OF HOLLY POND HIGH SCHOOL. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WAS NOTED ON THE SW CORNER OF THE BUILDING, AND A FEW BUSES HAD WINDOWS BLOWN IN THE PARKING LOT NE OF THIS LOCATION. THE TORNADO LIFTED SHORTLY THEREAFTER, WITH LITTLE/NO DEBRIS NOTED NE OF THE HIGH SCHOOL. SURVEY TEAMS DETERMINED WINDSPEEDS OF 80MPH ASSOCIATED WITH THIS BRIEF TORNADO.

MEASURED BY THE KHSV ASOS.

EF1 DAMAGE WAS FOUND ALONG AL HWY 69 IN JOPPA, SNAPPING AND UPPROOTING TREES, AND CAUSING MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A FEW STRUCTURES NORTH OF HWY 69. WINDS IN THIS LOCATION WERE FOUND TO BE 100MPH. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NE AND LIFTED ON A FARM ACROSS FROM WESTSIDE VETERINARY HOSPITAL IN HOG JAW.
TREES DOWN IN MADISON WITH A TREE ON AN AMBULANCE AT HWY 72/BALCH RD.

EF2 TORNADO DAMAGE WAS FOUND AT BRINDLEE MOUNTAIN PRIMARY SCHOOL IN UNION GROVE, AL. SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED AT THEIR BASE, AND THE SCHOOL ITSELF SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE ON THE SW SIDE. 10 CLASSROOMS, THE CAFETERIA, AND PORTIONS OF THE GYM WERE DESTROYED, RIPPING LARGE STEEL BOLTS AND STEEL PLATES FROM THE FOUNDATION, AND BENDING/TWISTING STEEL BEAMS SUPPORTING THE STRUCTURE. THE SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH 120MPH WINDS.
